core_mechanism: a feedback-augmented extremal bound in which the maximum extractable resource under measurement-based control equals a baseline potential difference plus the mutual information gained by the controller, so that acquired information relaxes the optimal bound at the cost of the resource spent acquiring/processing it
brief_reason: The abstract states the maximum extractable work is set by the free-energy difference plus a generalized mutual information between system and controller and can exceed the conventional (Carnot) bound, with consistency preserved because information processing itself costs work.
